title: Observability, Capacity, Flow Control, Alarms, and SLOs · Type-System Trace: Messaging Slo Alert
summary: A dashboard shows only queue depth, creates message-ID metric labels, ignores unacknowledged work and oldest age, and responds to memory alarms by adding publishers and increasing prefetch. Planned artifact: Add and verify the messaging slo alert increment in messaging telemetry, capacity, and SLO evidence board. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define availability, acceptance, routing, processing latency, age, loss, duplicate, poison, recovery, and capacity SLOs with symptom and cause alerts.
Trace memory and disk alarms, connection blocking, client blocked events, broker flow control, local publish drain, and upstream load shedding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Messaging Slo Alert · Theory Messaging Slo Alert
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 4493 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine backlog state, oldest age, SLO, and resource alarms into bounded operations action; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
